title: Garden Tool Storage Rack
summary: A wall-mounted 2x4 rack with staggered hanger blocks that holds long-handled garden tools by their handles, sorted by whether they have a D-ring grip.
description:
The layout step matters more than any cut here: lay your actual tools out over the 2x4 before drilling anything, hanging tools without a D-ring handle upside down at the top and tools with a D-ring hanging below. That sorting is what makes the rack work — a shovel with a D-ring needs to hang below the beam by its handle, while a rake without one gets slotted in from above.

This is designed to be customized rather than followed exactly: cut the main 2x4 to whatever length fits your space and your actual tool collection, and space the hanger blocks based on where your tools land when laid out, not a fixed measurement.

cutList.note: Cut to fit your space and tool collection.

steps.title: Measure and lay out your tools
steps.body: Measure your space and cut the main beam to your desired rack length. Lay the beam on a flat surface and place your actual tools over it — tools without a D-ring handle go upside down at the top, tools with a D-ring handle hang below. Mark the position of each hanger block based on where your tools land.

steps.title: Attach the upper hanger blocks
steps.body: Attach the upper row of hanger blocks to the top edge of the beam with screws, two per joint, leaving a 2" gap between blocks for sliding tools in.

steps.title: Add the lower hanger blocks
steps.body: Add the bottom row of hanger blocks.

steps.title: Mount the rack
steps.body: Locate two studs in the wall and hang the rack, high enough that your longest tool doesn't touch the ground. Use at least two screws per stud, four screws minimum.
